title: hieracium murorum subsp. vernantianum
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. vernantianum, also known as Wall Hawkweed, is a subspecies of the Hieracium murorum plant, which is a flowering plant in the dandelion family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. Hieracium murorum subsp. vernantianum can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion for its medicinal uses, although further research is necessary to confirm its efficacy.
